question_text: The asteroid belt lies between Mars and Jupiter. Scientists estimate there are millions of asteroids in the belt. Which statement best explains why these asteroids never combined to form a full planet?

options.label: A
options.text: The asteroids are moving too fast and collide with each other

options.label: B
options.text: Jupiter's strong gravity prevented the asteroids from gathering together

options.label: C
options.text: The Sun's gravity is too weak in the asteroid belt region

options.label: D
options.text: Asteroids are made of materials that repel each other

explanation: Jupiter's gravitational pull disturbed the asteroids as they formed, preventing them from accumulating into a single large body. Instead, the asteroids remained scattered throughout the belt.
